page: Amazon Web Services c7gn.16xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance powered by the custom 64-core AWS Graviton3 Neoverse-V1 processor built on the ARMv8 64-bit architecture. Operating at 2.6 GHz, the CPU features dedicated physical cores with one thread per core, eliminating hyperthreading overhead. The instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. It features no local storage or GPUs, relying entirely on network-attached storage. Network connectivity is a key highlight, delivering a baseline bandwidth of 200 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core capabilities and memory performance. The c7gn.16xlarge achieves top-tier results in Geekbench multi-core testing and software compilation with Clang, as well as in Passmark memory scores. Multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth, both cached and uncached, rank in the top 25%. Database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput also demonstrate strong performance. However, single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression tasks fall into the average category. Additionally, while medium-model LLM text generation is strong, text generation for small LLM models is poor, highlighting a performance tradeoff in low-parameter sequential inference tasks.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64 architecture and dedicated core allocation offer high compute density and memory bandwidth per resource unit, making it an efficient choice for scale-out workloads compared to traditional x86 architectures. Given the 200 Gbps network baseline and strong multi-core and memory performance, this instance is highly suited for network-intensive applications, high-performance computing, software compilation, containerized microservices, and distributed database operations.
description: Amazon Web Services c7gn.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by a 64-core AWS Graviton3 Neoverse-V1 ARM64 processor running at 2.6 GHz. It features 128.0 GB of memory, providing 2.0 GB per core, and lacks local storage and GPUs. The instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 200 Gbps. In benchmark evaluations, the c7gn.16xlarge demonstrates top-tier performance in software compilation and multi-core Geekbench tests, alongside strong memory bandwidth and database operations. However, single-core performance remains average, and small-model LLM text generation is poor. This server offers high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for multi-threaded, network-heavy workloads. It is best suited for network-intensive applications, software compilation, and distributed databases.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7gn.16xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized server with 64 Graviton3 cores, 128 GB RAM, and 200 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core and software compilation performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c7gn.16xlarge is a 64-core ARM64 compute-optimized instance with 128 GB RAM and 200 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A high-bandwidth, 64-core ARM64 compute instance designed for network-intensive workloads, software compilation, and distributed multi-threaded applications.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated ARM64 Graviton3 physical cores running at 2.6 GHz
128.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per core
Top-tier multi-core CPU and software compilation benchmark performance
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Suited for network-intensive applications, HPC, and distributed databases
